chunks.title: Introduction to New 5GC
chunks.summary: The discussion begins with an overview of New 5GC, a molecule that has been the subject of speculation regarding its potential health impacts. The speaker explains that it is not a new discovery and highlights its similarity to New 5AC, pointing out that humans lack the enzyme that produces New 5GC.
chunks.transcript: let's move on to talk about new 5gc as we wrap up this podcast so new 5gc seems to come up every few years when someone on YouTube does a video about it like it's a new thing but we've known about it for quite some time and it doesn't seem to be a problem for humans and I'll tell you why u5gc is a cyalic acid there is no new 5gc in our bodies there's an enzyme that makes new 5gc from a compound in our bodies that we do have new 5 AC called cmah as I mentioned this podcast that enzyme was lost two to three million years ago we don't fully understand why probably due to some sort of protection from infectious insults but new 5gc is very similar to new 5ac different in one atom and the hypothesis goes that because we don't have new 5G C in our bodies when we eat new 5gc in the meat that we are eating we develop antibodies to new 5gc and that could lead to increased rates of colon cancer or other cancers in

chunks.title: Research on New 5GC and Cancer Risk
chunks.summary: The speaker presents research findings indicating that there is no increased risk of colon cancer associated with New 5GC antibodies in kidney transplant recipients, debunking claims that it is harmful. Emphasizing controlled studies, they illustrate that such antibody exposure does not correlate with higher cancer rates.
chunks.transcript: there's some pretty interesting research looking at high levels of anti-nu5 GC antibodies in patients receiving kidney transplants I'm not sure how anyone can talk about new 5gc as a problem when they see papers like this one no increase in colon cancer risk following induction with new 5gc bearing Rabbit anti-t cell IGG in recipients of kidney transplants I'll break it down for you guys when people get kidney transplants in the United States they get Rabbit anti-t cell IGG which is a collection of immunoglobulins and there is new 5gc in there because it's from Rabbits who have new 5gc which leads to humans who are receiving these kidney transplants and get this anti-t cell IGG therapy to develop many more new 5gc antibodies and so we can look at that portion of the population in this controlled group of kidney transplant recipients and see that there is no increased risk of colon cancer in those people which essentially debunks the notion that new 5gc is harmful for humans at all as you can see here from the abstract we took advantage of the evidence that rabbit IGG elicits an immune response against new 5gc consulted a large database of allograft recipients that is kidney transplant recipients based on this data from 173 960 and 38 505 patients without and with atg induction respectively we found no evidence that exposure to higher levels of anti-new 5gc is associated with a higher incidence of colon cancer you'd probably have to eat a lot of red meat to get levels of anti-nu5 GC IGG or antibodies that these kidney transplant recipients had but nevertheless then the follow-up from them there was no increased rate of colon cancers

chunks.title: Comparison with Ferrets
chunks.summary: The segment compares human physiology with that of ferrets, which also lack New 5GC but consume prey containing it. The speaker mentions observations that ferrets do not suffer from increased cancer rates, further supporting the idea that New 5GC may not be detrimental to health.
chunks.transcript: Kingdom something that Western medicine doesn't do very often but I find quite revealing ferrets are part of a family of animals called mustolids and ferrets do not have new 5gc similar to humans they've also lost the ability to create that cylic acid from new 5ac or other precursor cyalic acids now ferrets eat animals with new 5gc in them they eat bowls and mice and rabbits perhaps animals with new 5gc so here we have a model of an animal like humans without new 5gc eating animals with new 5gc and according to the zoologists that I consult ferrets are not going extinct from cancers of any type due to this aberrant consumption of new 5gc in their prey so

chunks.title: Conclusion on New 5GC's Safety
chunks.summary: In closing, the speaker asserts that New 5GC is not harmful to humans and invites listeners to question unsubstantiated claims about its risks. They reflect on the historical aspect of human diets and evolution, arguing that if it were harmful, its long presence in the diet would suggest otherwise.
chunks.transcript: that point to the idea that new 5gc is not harmful for humans if you don't know what new 5gc is don't worry about it if you've heard about it and you've heard people say it's harmful for humans challenge them without evidence and see what comes out in the mix I don't think they'll have any answers for it why would something that has been at the center of the human diet for hundreds of thousands of years and prehombinant evolution was a critical factor in that
